commands.review_summary:
The PR prepends visible Signal quote context to `BodyForAgent` for quoted replies and suppresses the matching duplicate shared reply-target block in prompt metadata.

PR surface: Source +79, Tests +265. Total +344 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main can populate `ReplyToBody` while leaving `BodyForAgent` as only the new Signal message text. I did not run a live Signal roundtrip in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer needs to add redacted live Signal/device/daemon/terminal proof; there is no narrow code defect for repair automation.

target.title: fix(signal): include quoted reply context for agents
target.body:
Closes #98287
Related: #98234 (non-duplicate reply-session initialization conflict/drop issue)

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Signal users sending quoted replies could have the quoted target extracted into `ReplyToBody` metadata while the agent-facing `BodyForAgent` remained only the new ambiguous message, such as `Did this get done?`.

## Why This Change Was Made

Signal inbound handling now renders visible quoted-reply context directly into `BodyForAgent` as a clear blockquote while preserving clean `RawBody`, `CommandBody`, and `BodyForCommands` for command parsing and hooks. The shared inbound metadata renderer skips only the duplicate one-hop reply-target block when the Signal-generated wrapper exactly matches the expected quoted body and sender; richer `ReplyChain` context and ordinary user-authored blockquotes still render normally.

## User Impact

Signal quoted replies now carry the referenced message into agent-facing input, so agents can answer ambiguous followups using the intended quoted target. Plain non-quoted Signal messages remain unchanged, and quoted context is not duplicated in the model prompt when Signal has already inlined the generated quote block.

## Evidence

Duplicate search before filing found no open narrow issue/PR for `Signal quoted reply BodyForAgent` / `agent-facing body`; the closest existing items were closed #68665 and related-but-different #84364, plus broad open quote-reply PRs that do not provide this narrow BodyForAgent fix.

Validation run locally:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.inbound-context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts extensions/signal/src/inbound-context.contract.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo && node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src` (29 files, 375 tests)
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.inbound-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt 'Review the final Signal quoted-reply BodyForAgent change after exact generated-prefix duplicate checks that include expected sender and boundary. Confirm no accepted duplicate-context, equal-body, spoofed marker/altered wrapper, ReplyChain, or command/raw body regressions remain.'` clean with no accepted/actionable findings.

Caveat: no live Signal device roundtrip was run in this isolated checkout. The proof is source-level focused tests, the full Signal plugin Vitest directory, TypeScript checks, format/whitespace checks, and autoreview.

AI-assisted implementation; no agent transcript is included because this task involved private Signal context and the local transcript prompt flow was unavailable in this run mode.
target.author: joeykrug
